reasoning.issue: The question asks what test (e.g., ratio-based, CNI-based, etc.) underlies the Cumulative Amount / build-up basket and when the basket begins accruing capacity.
reasoning.analysis: The Cumulative Amount is a multi-source builder basket. Its overall aggregation uses a 'greatest_of' structure at the top level between (i) a three-test aggregate (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_three_test_aggregate) and (ii) a sum of individual additive sources. The three-test aggregate itself picks the greatest of: (a) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_cni), (b) the Available Retained ECF Amount — i.e., cumulative Excess Cash Flow not required to prepay term loans (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_ecf), and (c) 140% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_ebitda_fc). That greatest-of result is then added (sum aggregation) to: a starter amount of the greater of $130,000,000 or 100% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_starter), plus 100% of equity proceeds (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_other_equity_proceeds), retained asset sale proceeds (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds), investment returns (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_investment_returns), and debt-to-equity conversion amounts (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_other_debt_conversion). All cumulative sources share the same start anchor: the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs (growth_start_anchor = 'closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start'). The basket is reduced by RP usage, RDP usage, and investment usage (reduced_by_rp_usage, reduced_by_rdp_usage, reduced_by_investment_usage all true per the v3 entity).
reasoning.interactions: The three-test aggregate (6e76ed06_builder_source_three_test_aggregate) contributes via 'greatest_of' to the top-level usage permission (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission) at child_index 0, while the starter, equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, and debt conversion sources contribute via 'sum' at child_indices 1, 5, 6, 7, and 8 respectively. Asset sale event proceeds flow into 6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds when retained under the leverage-based sweep exemptions. The builder basket is also shared with the RDP basket (6e76ed06_builder_rdp_basket_permission, shares_with_rp_builder: true), subject to intercreditor/subordination restrictions.
reasoning.conclusion: The Cumulative Amount basket uses a 'greatest of' three-way earnings/cash-flow test (50% CNI vs. Available Retained ECF vs. 140% LTM EBITDA) as its core income-driven component, plus a fixed/grower starter amount and additive equity/asset/return sources, and begins accruing from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs.

stage2.answer: The Cumulative Amount (build-up basket) is not a single-ratio test but a multi-source structure with a 'greatest of' earnings core: the basket selects the largest of (i) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income, (ii) the Available Retained ECF Amount (cumulative Excess Cash Flow not required to prepay Term Loans under Section 2.10(f)), or (iii) 140% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA, and then adds to that result a starter amount equal to the greater of $130,000,000 or 100% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA, plus 100% of equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, and debt-to-equity conversion amounts [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32]. The basket begins growing from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32]. Capacity is reduced dollar-for-dollar by RP, RDP, and investment usage, and the same basket is shared with the RDP builder basket (subject to intercreditor/subordination restrictions) [Section 6.09(a)(A), p. 199].

questions.gold_answer: The Cumulative Amount is based on the greatest of three tests: (1) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income (which amount shall not be less than zero for any fiscal quarter), (2) Excess Cash Flow not required to be applied to prepay Term Loans or any other debt (such amount cannot be less than $0 for any fiscal year), (3) cumulative Consolidated EBITDA minus 140% of cumulative Consolidated Fixed Charges. All tests start growing from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs.

reasoning.issue: Whether the Borrower is permitted under the restricted payments covenant to distribute (by dividend or otherwise) equity interests it holds in Unrestricted Subsidiaries.
reasoning.analysis: Section 6.06(p) contains a specific categorical permission for 'the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of, or Indebtedness owed to Holdings or a Restricted Subsidiary by, or any assets of, an Unrestricted Subsidiary or the proceeds thereof.' The extracted v3 entity attributes confirm: (i) covers_equity_interests = true, (ii) is_categorical = true, (iii) capacity_category = 'categorical', and (iv) requires_valid_designation = false. This means the permission is not subject to a dollar cap, leverage ratio test, or builder basket availability — it is an unconditional categorical carve-out from the general RP prohibition. There are no defeaters listed against this norm. The norm binds the borrower_party and scopes the action as make_dividend_payment with object unrestricted_subsidiary_equity_or_assets. The J.Crew blocker norm (6e76ed06_jcrew_blocker_prohibition) applies only to designation of Restricted Subsidiaries as Unrestricted Subsidiaries (covering Material Intellectual Property), not to the act of distributing equity already held in an existing Unrestricted Subsidiary, so it does not limit Section 6.06(p). The supplementary norms (builder basket, general RP basket, ratio basket) provide separate avenues for cash dividends but are not needed for this categorical permission.
reasoning.interactions: The J.Crew blocker (6e76ed06_jcrew_blocker_prohibition) governs designation of Restricted Subsidiaries as Unrestricted Subsidiaries and does not defeat or qualify the Section 6.06(p) distribution permission. No defeaters are listed against 6e76ed06_unrestricted_sub_equity_distribution_permission. The builder basket norm notes rebuilds_builder_basket = true for the unsub distribution basket, meaning distributions under 6.06(p) can rebuild the Cumulative Amount, but this is a benefit, not a restriction.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes — Section 6.06(p) contains a categorical, uncapped permission for the Borrower to distribute (by dividend or otherwise) equity interests of Unrestricted Subsidiaries, with no leverage test, dollar cap, or other condition applicable.

stage2.answer: Yes. Section 6.06(p) [p. 198] expressly and categorically permits 'the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of, or Indebtedness owed to Holdings or a Restricted Subsidiary by, or any assets of, an Unrestricted Subsidiary or the proceeds thereof.' This permission is unconditional — the extracted data confirms it is categorical (is_categorical = true, capacity_category = 'categorical') and requires no valid designation condition, no dollar cap, and no leverage ratio test. No defeaters apply to this norm. Note that the J.Crew blocker [Section 'Definition of Unrestricted Subsidiary', p. 89] restricts designation of new Unrestricted Subsidiaries that hold Material Intellectual Property, but does not affect the right to distribute equity of an already-designated Unrestricted Subsidiary under 6.06(p).

reasoning.analysis: Three reallocation pathways are relevant: (1) The general RDP basket under Section 6.09(a)(I) has cap_usd=$130M / 100% Consolidated EBITDA and action_scope='reallocable', matching the general RP basket under Section 6.06(j) (same cap_usd=$130M / 100% EBITDA, action_scope='reallocable', capacity_composition='fungible'). These two baskets share the same dollar sizing, and the general RP basket's 'fungible' capacity_composition flag indicates they draw from a shared pool — capacity used under the RDP basket reduces what is available under the RP basket and vice versa. (2) The builder/Cumulative Amount basket (Section 'Definition of Cumulative Amount', p.32) is expressly shared across RP uses (dividends, equity repurchases), RDP uses (subordinated debt prepayment), and investment uses — the v3 attributes confirm reduced_by_rp_usage=true, reduced_by_rdp_usage=true, and reduced_by_investment_usage=true. The builder_rdp_basket (Section 6.09(a)(A)) explicitly shares_with_rp_builder=true, meaning usage of the Cumulative Amount for RDP draws down the same pool available for RP. Investment returns that flow back into the Cumulative Amount (builder_source_investment_returns) similarly feed the shared pool available for dividends. (3) The ratio RDP basket (Section 6.09(a)(J)) is unlimited_conditional (First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x or no-worse test), but its capacity_category is unlimited_conditional — it is not reallocable to RP in the general sense. No investment basket reallocation entities (basket_reallocates_to links) appear in the data beyond the Cumulative Amount mechanics. The data does not show a standalone investment basket with a direct reallocation link to the RP covenant beyond the builder basket.
reasoning.interactions: The general RP basket (6.06(j)) and general RDP basket (6.09(a)(I)) share a fungible pool at the same $130M/100% EBITDA cap — usage under one reduces the other. The Cumulative Amount/builder basket is the primary cross-covenant shared pool: reduced_by_rp_usage, reduced_by_rdp_usage, and reduced_by_investment_usage are all true, so any usage for investments or RDP draws down capacity available for dividends and vice versa. Asset sale proceeds retained after sweep flow into the builder basket via builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds, further linking the asset sale/investment ecosystem to RP capacity.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes — there are two primary reallocation paths: (a) the general RDP basket (Section 6.09(a)(I)) shares a fungible $130M/100% EBITDA pool with the general RP basket (Section 6.06(j)), and (b) the Cumulative Amount/builder basket is a single shared pool drawn down by RP, RDP, and investment usage alike, with investment returns and retained asset-sale proceeds feeding back into that same pool.

stage2.answer: Yes, there are two key reallocation paths. First, the general RDP basket under Section 6.09(a)(I) (greater of $130M and 100% Consolidated EBITDA) is fungible with the general RP basket under Section 6.06(j) (same sizing) — both have action_scope='reallocable' and share a single capacity pool, so capacity not used for debt prepayments can be used for restricted payments/dividends [Section 6.06(j), p.196; Section 6.09(a)(I), p.200]. Second, the Cumulative Amount (builder basket, Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32) is a shared pool reduced by RP usage, RDP usage, and investment usage alike — meaning investment returns (builder_source_investment_returns), retained asset-sale proceeds (builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds), and equity proceeds (builder_source_other_equity_proceeds) all build capacity in the same pool available for dividends; conversely, investments or RDP payments drawn from the builder basket reduce what is available for restricted payments [Section 6.09(a)(A), p.199; Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32]. No standalone investment basket with a direct one-way reallocation link to the RP covenant (separate from the builder basket mechanics) is present in the extracted data.

reasoning.issue: The question asks whether asset sale proceeds can be used to fund dividend payments under the credit agreement's restricted payments covenant.
reasoning.analysis: The key pathway is through the Cumulative Amount (builder basket). Norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds is a dedicated builder source that adds retained asset sale proceeds to the Cumulative Amount (child_index 6, summed into the builder basket under 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission). The proceeds_flow edge confirms that Net Cash Proceeds from asset sales subject to mandatory prepayment under Section 2.10(c) flow into this clause when retained — i.e., when they are not required to be swept under the leverage-based tier exemptions or fall below de minimis thresholds. Once added to the Cumulative Amount, those proceeds become part of the general_purpose builder basket capacity, which explicitly scopes make_dividend_payment as a permitted action. The builder basket also aggregates other sources (starter amount of greater of $130M/100% EBITDA, 50% CNI, retained ECF, 140% EBITDA forward commitment, 100% equity proceeds, investment returns, debt conversion) on a sum/greatest-of basis. Separately, dividends are also permitted under: (1) the general RP basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission) at the greater of $130M or 100% Consolidated EBITDA [Section 6.06(j)], without any specific asset-sale-proceeds nexus; and (2) the ratio basket (6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission) on an unlimited basis if First Lien Leverage Ratio is ≤5.75x or no worse than the prior test period on a pro forma basis [Section 6.06(o)]. Additionally, proceeds of Unrestricted Subsidiary assets may be distributed as dividends categorically [Section 6.06(p)]. No defeaters are present in the data.
reasoning.interactions: Asset sale proceeds feed into the builder basket (6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds → 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission via sum aggregation, child_index 6). The builder basket capacity, once built up by retained asset sale proceeds, is consumed when dividends are paid, reducing the available Cumulative Amount (reduced_by_rp_usage = true). The general RP basket and ratio basket operate independently of the asset-sale-proceeds builder source and do not depend on the proceeds flow. No defeaters override any of these permissions.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes, asset sale proceeds that are retained (i.e., not required to be swept under the mandatory prepayment provisions) accumulate in the Cumulative Amount builder basket and can be used to make dividends under that basket. Additionally, dividends may be made under the general RP basket, ratio basket, or unrestricted subsidiary distribution basket without any direct asset-sale-proceeds nexus.

stage2.answer: Yes. Retained asset sale proceeds (those not required to be applied to mandatory prepayment under Section 2.10(c) due to leverage-based sweep tier exemptions or de minimis thresholds) flow into the Cumulative Amount builder basket as a dedicated additive source and, once accumulated there, may be used to make dividend payments [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32; builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds, child_index 6]. Separately and independently, dividends may also be made using the general RP basket (greater of $130,000,000 or 100% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period) [Section 6.06(j), p.196], or on an unlimited basis if First Lien Leverage Ratio is ≤5.75x (or no worse than the prior test period) on a pro forma basis [Section 6.06(o), p.198], or by distributing assets (including proceeds thereof) of an Unrestricted Subsidiary [Section 6.06(p), p.198] — none of which require asset sale proceeds as a funding source.

GENERAL PURPOSE (summable fixed-dollar floors):
1. Section 6.06(j) General RP Basket [6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission]: greater of $130,000,000 or 100% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period. Fixed floor = $130M.
2. Builder/Cumulative Amount [6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission]: general_purpose basket. The Cumulative Amount is computed as: Starter Amount (greater of $130M or 100% LTM Consolidated EBITDA) PLUS the greatest of: (a) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income since closing-date fiscal quarter start, (b) Available Retained ECF Amount (cumulative retained Excess Cash Flow), or (c) 140% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA at the Reference Date — PLUS 100% of equity proceeds received + retained asset sale proceeds + investment returns + debt conversion proceeds. The fixed-floor starter = $130M. All other components are variable (formula-based) and not quantifiable without current financial data.

No shares_capacity_pool or basket_reallocates_to links are present between the General RP Basket and the Builder Basket, so they are independent pools. Each general_purpose basket is counted separately.

Step 2 — Fixed-dollar quantifiable floors (general_purpose):
- General RP Basket floor: $130,000,000
- Builder Basket starter floor: $130,000,000 (greater of $130M or 100% LTM EBITDA)
Total fixed-dollar general_purpose floor = $130M + $130M = $260,000,000

All other builder sources (50% CNI, retained ECF, 140% EBITDA, equity proceeds, asset sale proceeds, investment returns, debt conversion) are variable and not quantifiable without current financials.

Step 3 — RESTRICTED PURPOSE:
- Management Equity Basket [6e76ed06_management_equity_basket_permission]: greater of $20M/yr or 15% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA; carryforward permitted. Restricted to mgmt equity repurchases. Fixed floor = $20M/yr.

Step 4 — UNLIMITED CONDITIONAL:
- Ratio Basket [6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission]: unlimited dividends if First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x (or no-worse test). Not dollar-quantifiable.

Step 5 — CATEGORICAL (no dollar cap):
- Tax distributions [6e76ed06_tax_distribution_basket_permission]: categorical, actual tax liability.
- HoldCo overhead [6e76ed06_holdco_overhead_basket_permission]: categorical, actual overhead.

reasoning.issue: Whether the Borrower can dividend a $200m asset/business division with negative EBITDA to shareholders when the First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 6.0x, focusing on available RP baskets.
reasoning.analysis:
There are four potential pathways to analyze:

1. RATIO BASKET (Section 6.06(o), norm 6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission): This basket permits unlimited dividends on a pro forma basis if the First Lien Leverage Ratio is no greater than the GREATER OF (x) 5.75x OR (y) the pre-transaction First Lien Net Leverage Ratio. The absolute threshold is 5.75x. The no-worse test compares pro forma leverage to pre-transaction leverage. Pre-transaction leverage is stated at 6.0x. The key question is the pro forma impact. Since the divested business has negative EBITDA, removing it from the consolidated group INCREASES Consolidated EBITDA (the denominator), which IMPROVES (decreases) the leverage ratio on a pro forma basis. Therefore, pro forma First Lien Net Leverage Ratio will be LESS THAN 6.0x. The no-worse test requires pro forma leverage ≤ pre-transaction leverage (6.0x). Since pro forma leverage is below 6.0x (improved by removal of negative-EBITDA assets), the no-worse test is SATISFIED. The condition_tree is an OR: either (a) ≤5.75x absolute, or (b) no-worse. Branch (b) is satisfied. The ratio basket is available.

2. GENERAL RP BASKET (Section 6.06(j), norm 6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission): Permits dividends up to the greater of $130m or 100% of Consolidated EBITDA. The object is a $200m asset, so this basket would be insufficient in isolation unless fair market value is not the measure, but it could partially apply. However, the ratio basket already provides unlimited capacity.

3. BUILDER BASKET (norm 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission): General purpose basket growing from Closing Date; available but subject to its own conditions. No leverage condition is specified here in the extracted data, so this could be an additional avenue, but the ratio basket resolves the question.

4. UNRESTRICTED SUB BASKET (Section 6.06(p), norm 6e76ed06_unrestricted_sub_equity_distribution_permission): This is a categorical permission for distributing assets OF an Unrestricted Subsidiary. The question describes a business division of the Borrower (a Restricted entity), not an Unrestricted Subsidiary, so this carve-out does not appear directly applicable unless the division is first designated as an Unrestricted Subsidiary.

Because the business has negative EBITDA, its removal improves consolidated EBITDA and thus improves the leverage ratio pro forma below the current 6.0x — satisfying the no-worse branch even though 6.0x exceeds the 5.75x absolute threshold. No defeaters are present in the data. The business_division object class is explicitly within the scope of the ratio basket.
reasoning.conclusion: The Borrower can dividend the $200m business division to shareholders at 6.0x First Lien Net Leverage Ratio using the ratio basket under Section 6.06(o), because the no-worse test is satisfied: removing a negative-EBITDA business improves the pro forma leverage ratio below the current 6.0x pre-transaction level.
